Prunus serotina var. susquehanae is a variety of the Black Cherry tree (Prunus serotina), a type of deciduous tree native to eastern North America.

The Black Cherry tree has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The leaves and bark of the tree have been used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.

The bioactive constituents of Prunus serotina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made from Prunus serotina include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
